text: Risk management is a key process in any project or business. It begins with identifying risks, which means looking for anything that could go wrong, such as budget cuts or technical failures. Once risks are identified, teams create mitigation strategies to reduce their impact; for example, they might set aside extra time for difficult tasks. However, not all risks can be avoided, so contingency plans are also needed. These are backup actions to take if a problem actually occurs. Finally, monitoring risks throughout the project is crucial. By regularly checking for new issues and reviewing existing ones, teams can adjust their plans and stay prepared for unexpected changes.
